core_thesis: Models are useful for analysis, extraction, summarization, and option generation, but too brittle to trust as the final execution gate for high-stakes action-boundary decisions.

what_holoverify_is.plain_english: HoloVerify is a governed decision architecture that asks whether a high-stakes action may proceed now using only visible source records.
not: not a claim that models stop being fallible

action_boundary_definitions.procurement: May the organization create, change, approve, or pay this vendor, purchase order, payment rail, or release request now?
action_boundary_definitions.legal: May the organization sign, release, settle, disclose, waive, or rely on this legal authority now?
action_boundary_definitions.finance: May the organization move money, recognize revenue, change bank details, approve an expense, or close a financial control now?
action_boundary_definitions.clinical: May the organization activate treatment, start a protocol, administer medication, release a result, or rely on clinical clearance now?
action_boundary_definitions.security: May the organization grant access, change a privileged role, disable a control, execute containment, or act on an incident now?
action_boundary_definitions.contracts: May the organization bind itself to this clause, renewal, amendment, order, discount, termination, or approval now?

solo_model_failure_modes.name: false_allow_underblock
solo_model_failure_modes.plain_english: The model says go ahead when a required approval, scope, amount, identity, timing, or authority field is missing.

solo_model_failure_modes.name: false_escalation_overblock
solo_model_failure_modes.plain_english: The model blocks a valid action because it sounds sensitive even though visible records close the boundary.

solo_model_failure_modes.name: parse_admissibility_brittleness
solo_model_failure_modes.plain_english: The model answers in the wrong shape, omits required fields, or produces prose that cannot be safely consumed.

solo_model_failure_modes.name: vendor_inconsistency
solo_model_failure_modes.plain_english: Different model families reach different answers or rely on different facts for the same packet.

solo_model_failure_modes.name: equal_verdicts_unequal_reasons
solo_model_failure_modes.plain_english: Two models may give the same verdict while one is source-grounded and the other is guessing from tone or adjacent authority.

why_90_to_96_percent_is_not_enough.plain_english: At an action boundary, a 4-10 percent miss rate can mean several serious errors per 100 high-stakes decisions.
examples: payments released to the wrong account or without payment-rail authority
vendor-master changes approved from a plausible request instead of current source records
treatment or protocol activation started from scheduling or triage evidence that does not authorize treatment
contract changes treated as binding without the required clause, signer, scope, or approval
privileged access granted without the right tenant, environment, asset, or approval

what_changes_when_holo_is_added.structured_worker_gov_turns: Workers produce constrained artifacts and Gov carries bounded state forward.
what_changes_when_holo_is_added.source_boundary_discipline: The system keeps asking whether visible sources close the exact requested action boundary.
what_changes_when_holo_is_added.deterministic_gates: Known source-field checks can fail closed before prose or consensus can hide the mistake.
what_changes_when_holo_is_added.blocker_ledgers: Once a source-grounded blocker appears, later turns must preserve it or close it with specific source fields.
what_changes_when_holo_is_added.posthoc_traceability: Prompts, outputs, trace files, provider calls, and scoring are preserved for audit.
what_changes_when_holo_is_added.failure_preservation_and_patch_regressio: Failed runs are preserved, failure classes are named, mechanism-level gates are added, and bounded lanes are rerun.

analogy.name: surgical_timeout
analogy.text: A skilled surgeon matters, but surgery also needs patient identity checks, site marking, allergy checks, instrument counts, role separation, and a written record. HoloVerify treats the model as one participant inside a controlled procedure, not as the whole safety system.

patching_language.safe: We discovered failure classes, converted them into mechanism-level gates, preserved the failed evidence, and reran bounded validation lanes to check whether the mechanism repaired that class.
patching_language.principle: A mechanism patch learns a general control rule; it does not memorize packet answers. The claim remains bounded to the lane tested.

unsafe_language: HoloVerify is universally superior to solo models.
HoloVerify has solved all action-boundary risk.
HoloVerify proves global FNR or FPR reduction from internal selected lanes.
V6 proves FP precision or broad benchmark superiority.
The old 614 and current blind-120 evidence can be combined into one public denominator.
Patch-validation evidence is public benchmark evidence.

claim_boundary.safe_framing: HoloVerify is an architecture for testing whether governed, source-bound, traceable AI procedures can reduce known solo-model failure classes at high-stakes action boundaries.
claim_boundary.old_614: stale/historical unless re-admitted under current rules
claim_boundary.v6_tier3_and_broader_v6: internal selected-lane or internal validation evidence only

recommendation: Use this story for HoloOps and Taylor alignment before editing public pages.
Keep public copy centered on the action-boundary question, not on model-vs-model spectacle.
Keep patch language mechanism-level and bounded.
Keep public claims conservative until a separate public-claim audit approves stronger language.
